contraventionDescription: Parked after expiry of time paid for in a pay and display car park. Contravention Code 82

sections.heading: How to have your penalty charge notice (PCN) for this parking contravention cancelled.
sections.content: We may be able to assist you in having your penalty charge notice for this contravention cancelled.
sections.contentHtml: We may be able to assist you in having your penalty charge notice for this contravention cancelled.

sections.heading: Follow our 3 stage process:
sections.content:
1 Check: Specific grounds for appeal for this contravention (below on this page)
This may also include diagrams and pictures showing signage requirements etc.
2 Check: General grounds for appeal
Applies to most contraventions.
3 Check: Summarised parking adjudicators decisions
Includes details of example cases won.
sections.contentHtml:
1 Check: Specific grounds for appeal for this contravention (below on this page)
This may also include diagrams and pictures showing signage requirements etc.

2 Check: General grounds for appeal
Applies to most contraventions.

3 Check: Summarised parking adjudicators decisions
Includes details of example cases won.

sections.heading: Specific grounds of appeal for this contravention
sections.content:
1. Are the instructions clearly visible and understandable on the pay and display machine? If the controlled hours are not on the signs were you able to see the details on the machine. Was it dark and a streetlight not working? The local authority has an obligation to make sure that the hours of enforcement and the signs advising that you are in a pay and display car park and the terms and conditions are clearly visible. If they are not then you should appeal. Also check that the charge fee shown and the penalty on the signs and machines is the same as sometimes machines/signs are not updated with the new charges or indeed the new fines for non compliance .If there is any discrepancy then you should appeal.
Below we show diagrams of the signs which must be in situ by law and much more information that will help you decide whether to appeal against your penalty charge notice.
2. You must move your car before the expiry time purchased has expired. Was the ticket issued before the expiry time shown on the pay and display ticket, if so the practice of issuing penalty charge notices before the time has expired does happen. Your pay and display ticket is your proof. You should send in a photocopy of the ticket or photograph to the council with your representation.
3. We have heard of instances where motorists, who have received a PCN, then borrow a ticket from someone else and send it in by way of proof that they were issued with a penalty charge notice incorrectly.  Of course we can not condone such practices.
4. If you believe the machine to be defective, ask the local authority for a copy of the maintenance record for that machine as any previously reported records of malfunction should be logged.
5.) If you paid using a phone and the payment did not go through then you should appeal. Your phone logs calls made and usually the duration of the call otherwise your service provider should be able to provide you with proof of the call.
6.) Remember that you can always cite compassionate grounds when making a representation.
Please see our section on parking adjudicators decisions which will have details of cases for this contravention.
 
Below: Signs that should be clearly visible in the car park. Please note the permitted variations and any sizes shown.
Back to Parking
